description_long: This object is a roughly cylindrical stone with a slightly irregular, blocky shape and a coarse, granular surface texture. Its coloration is predominantly a pale, dusty beige with subtle hints of pinkish and grayish undertones, suggesting natural mineral variation. The surface is pitted and uneven, with small indentations and weathered marks that give it a rugged, timeworn appearance. The top and bottom faces are somewhat flattened but not perfectly level, while the sides are rounded yet still display angular facets. There are no visible carvings, inscriptions, or artificial modifications, indicating it is likely a naturally occurring or minimally processed stone. Its size and form make it suitable as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments such as parks, sidewalks, or plazas.
description: A pale, roughly cylindrical stone with a coarse, pitted surface and subtle color variations, appearing rugged and naturally weathered.
description_view_0: This view shows a slightly angular side with pronounced surface pitting and a rounded top edge.
description_view_1: This view highlights the flattest top surface and the most uniform, cylindrical side profile.
description_view_2: This view presents a more blocky, vertical face with visible granular texture and subtle color shifts.
description_view_3: This view reveals a rounded corner and a smoother transition between the top and side surfaces.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or barrier on pavement or soil
attribute_pattern: Irregular granular and pitted texture with no regular pattern or artificial markings; subtle mottling from mineral content.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with visible pitting and minor surface erosion, indicating prolonged exposure to outdoor elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically contrasts with smooth man-made surfaces but may blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; can be partially surrounded by soil or low vegetation.
